Notice Of Motion For An Order Confirming The Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Nov-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Grant Baker, Esq. representing Plaintiff Alma R. Campos
Jessica Perl, Court Appointed Referree

Re Motion confirming the sale of Real Property

The Court appointed referree conducts the sale of the property located at 2711-2713 Harrison Street, San Francisco, California and states the terms and conditions of the sale.

The sale price of the property is $1,200,000.00.

There is no other appearances for an overbid of the sale price.

The Court confirms the sale price of $1,200,000.00

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the court confirming the sale of the property that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-13-2024 Continued To Jan-08-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Declaration Of Plaintiff Alma R. Campos In Support Of Request For Default (Court) Judgment Against Defendants David Garcia And Raul Garcia
Off Calendar May-29-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for a request for an interlocutory judgment against Defendant David Garcia and Raul Garcia.

Dept.505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" only to be used in compliance with California Government Code Section 69957, commencing at 9:39:04 a.m., pausing at 10:02:39 am., resuming at 11:24:18 a.m., concluding at 11:28:06 a.m. (D505)

Christopher A. Klapperich, Esq. representing Plaintiff Alma R. Campos via Zoom video conference.
Plaintiff Alma R. Campos was placed under oath and testified via Zoom video conference.
Defendants David Garcia and Raul Garcia not appearing.

Plaintiff's complaint for quiet title filed May-18-2024.
Proof of personal service to Defendants on Jul-25-2023.
Default entered as to Defendants on Aug-29-2023.
Dismissal filed as to All persons unknown claiming any interest in the property and Does 1-10 on Feb-01-2024.

Counsel to summarize the cause/s of action.
The Court reviews the proposed judgment and reads into the record each request delineated.
The Court inquires as to No.10(b) on the proposed judgment.
Counsel to clarify, cites CCP 874.040 and to provide proof as to the request.
Court examines Plaintiff.
Plaintiff confirms that counsel statements are true and correct. Plaintiff confirms that the declarations submitted are true and correct.
Counsel confers with Plaintiff on the record re: attorney's fees and Plaintiff agrees.
Plaintiff testifies that all liens against the subject property are paid.
The Court passes the matter to allow counsel to amend the proposed judgment for specificity of the order.
Proceedings resume Counsel and plaintiff are present via zoom video conference.

Based on the testimony, statements made, exhibits presented and declarations, the Court finds in favor of the plaintiff and GRANTS judgment against the defendants as follows:
1.The subject of this action is the real property (hereinafter the "Real Property") located in the City and County of San Francisco, State of California, commonly known as 2711 - 2713 Harrison Street, San Francisco, California and legally described as follows:
BEGINNING AT A POINT ON THE EASTERLY LINE OF HARRISON STREET, DISTANT THEREON 52 FEET SOUTHERLY FROM THE SOUTHERLY LINE OF 23RD STREET, RUNNING THENCE SOUTHERLY ALONG SAID LINE OF HARRISON STREET 26 FEET; THENCE AT A RIGHT ANGLE EASTERLY 75 FEET; THENCE AT A RIGHT ANGLE NORTHERLY 26 FEET; THENCE AT A RIGHT ANGLE WESTERLY 75 FEET TO THE POINT OF BEGINNING.

BEING A PORTION OF MISSION BLOCK NO. 151 [Assessor's Lot 32; Block 4206]

2.The owners of the fee simple interest in the Real Property are:
Name: Ownership Interest:
Alma Rose Campos One-third (1/3)
David Garcia One-third (1/3)
Raul Garcia One-third (1/3)
3.The Real Property is not subject to any encumbrances.
4.Sale of the Real Property and division of the net proceeds of sale is more equitable than division of the Real Property in kind.
5.The Real Property shall be sold and the net proceeds of the sale applied in accordance with the provisions set forth herein.
6.Michael Valencia, Esq. of Ross, Hackett, Dowling and Valencia is appointed referee with authority to sell the Real Property at private sale, to the highest and best net bidder, subject to confirmation of the Court, which manner of sale is hereby determined to be most beneficial to the parties. Notice of sale shall be given in the manner required for notice of sale specified in California Code of Civil Procedure 873.640. The sale of the Real Property shall be conducted as provided for in California Code of Civil Procedure 873.600-873.690 and otherwise in accordance with all applicable laws. No bond shall be required of the referee until such time as the sale of the Real Property is confirmed by the Court and at the discretion of the Court.
--See scanned interlocutory judgment---

The proposed judgment is signed and executed.
